mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show positive direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 52%, or interaction term coefficient turns negative in regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity utilization approaching limits, prompting anticipatory equity positioning that yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum generates negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of late-cycle observations, or the mechanism reverses direction during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenue projections and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth remains below 0.01, or forward 20-day returns are negative in >55% of months with positive payroll revisions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly core inflation reading exceeding 2.5% forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in >60% of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and industrial production confirms synchronized macroeconomic growth, which reduces recession risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60-day windows, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with a tightening federal funds rate during a monetary contraction cycle signals stagflationary pressure, which triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive 5-day returns in >50% of tightening cycle observations, or the mechanism fails to separate from single-factor effects in multivariate regression.
